error_signature: decimal field not showing trailing zeros
title: Decimal field not displaying two decimal places
symptom: When using a decimal field in a text formula, trailing zeros are not displayed for values without hundredths. For example, 20.5 displays as 20.5 instead of 20.50.
applies_when: Using a decimal field from Xano
Applying a text formula with rounding
root_cause: The text formula in WeWeb does not automatically format decimal values to show trailing zeros when the hundredths place is zero. This can lead to inconsistencies in how currency values are displayed.
fix_steps: Use the rounding function in your text formula to round the number to two decimal places.
Apply the JavaScript method: return (Math.round(number * 100) / 100).toFixed(2) to ensure two decimal places are displayed.
agent_instruction: Implement the JavaScript rounding method in your text formula to display two decimal places.
